neapolitan-pastiera-pequerecetas.name: Neapolitan Pastiera

ingredients.item: 200 g de trigo cocido (previamente remojado durante la noche)
1 cucharadita de esencia de flor de azahar

instructions: In a big bowl, mix flour with butter and sugar up to a sandy texture. Add the eggs and amasa to form a smooth and homogenous mass. Wrap the dough into clear film and let it stand in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.

In a big bowl, mix ricotta with sugar until well integrated. Add the eggs one by one, and mix well after each addition. It incorporates steamed wheat, lemon frying and the essence of azahar flower. Mix up and get a uniform blend. If you wish, you can add condado fruits and a pinch of cinnamon powder or azahar water to give more flavour to the pilling. Fold well.

It purifies the oven to 180 been 3 been (177 been 3). Stretch the mass with a roller onto an enlarged surface and get a thickness of about 0,5 cm. Fold a cake mold with a stretched mass, making sure you cover both the bottom and edges. Fold the ricotta and wheat mixture onto the dough.

With the remaining mass, pull again with a roller onto a pitted surface and get a thickness of about 0,5 cm. With a sharp knife or cutting wheel, cut strips of mass about 1 cm wide and long enough to cover the surface of the passing onto a grid. place cross-mass strips onto the surface of the pastiera, and form a pounding pattern. With the ends of the strips, press gently at the edges of the base mass to seal them. If you wish, you can brush the dough strips with beaten egg to give them a golden glow at billing.
Hornea passed it in the preset oven for about 60-70 minutes, or until the surface is golden and firm to the touch.
Once baked, remove the passing from the oven and let completely cool before crumbling.
Sprinkle sugars above before serving.

nectarine-lemonade.name: Nectarine Lemonade
instructions: Place all the ingredients in a blender. if it looks too thick, you can add some more  lemonade if you’d like. Blend well.
Then pour into two glasses with ıce and enjoy .

nestle-toll-house-mint-filled-delightful.name: Nestle® Toll House® Mint Filled Delightfulls™ Chocolate Chip Cookies

ingredients.item: Nestle® toll house® delightfulls mint filled morsels

instructions: Preheat the oven to 191°C (190 degrees C).

Combine flour, baking soda, and salt in a small bowl. Beat butter, white sugar, brown sugar, and vanilla in large mixer bowl until creamy. Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition. Gradually beat in flour mixture. Stir in DelightFulls® morsels and nuts.
Drop by rounded tablespoon onto ungreased baking sheets. Bake until golden brown, 9 to 11 minutes. Cool on baking sheets for 2 minutes, then move to wire racks to cool completely.

ninoise-salad-with-fresh-salmon-argiro.name: Ninoise Salad With Fresh Salmon
instructions: Salt and pepper the salmon fillets and place them in parchment paper.
Wrap them carefully and hardly in a folder and baking for 15 minutes in the oven, at 200°C.
Heat the beans in salted water for 5 minutes.
Then drain them well.
Cut the lettuce in a salad pot.
Add the tomatoes cut in half, drained beans, olives and potatoes.
Remove the salmon from the parchment paper and cut it into small bites.
Beat the ingredients for the dressing in the blender and then pour with this salad.
Mix well, go everywhere, and serve.

no-cook-peach-mousse.name: No-cook Peach Mousse

ingredients.prep: or 12 ounces frozen sliced peaches, thawed

ingredients.item: Plus  0.25 cup peach or apricot jam

instructions:
Pour ¼ cup bourbon into a small saucepan and sprinkle 1 packet gelatin over. Let soak to soften gelatin, about 10 minutes.
→  Wait, what is gelatin, anyways?

Meanwhile, score skins of 1 lb. peaches with a sharp knife in a large “X” pattern on pointy (non-stem) end and place in a colander in sink. (If you’re using frozen peaches, skip this and proceed with step 5.)
Heat a kettle (or saucepan) full of water until water boils, then pour over peaches to loosen skin. Rinse peaches in cold water.
Slip blade of a paring knife underneath loosened cross-hatched peach skin and peel back to remove. If peels are stubbornly stuck, use a vegetable peeler. Cut peaches into wedges; discard pits.
Blend peach flesh, ⅔ cup jam, ¼ cup mint leaves, and 2 Tbsp. lemon juice in a blender on highest speed until smooth. Leave blender jar on the base because you’re about to come back to it.

Gently heat saucepan with gelatin mixture over low, swirling, just until granules dissolve and mixture is translucent. Don’t blast the heat because this can destroy the setting power of the gelatin and it might ignite the bourbon! Keep it low.
Stream gelatin mixture into blender with the motor on low speed.

Transfer peach mixture to a large bowl and chill in freezer, stirring with a spatula every 5 minutes, until mixture is thick enough that you can see the bottom of bowl as you stir (be sure to scrape against sides and bottom of bowl with spatula where mixture has become more solid), 15–20 minutes total. As it cools, gelatin will start to set and mixture will thicken.

While peach mixture hangs out in freezer, whip 1½ cups cream and a pinch of salt in another large bowl with a whisk. You can do this in a mixer, but in a large bowl starting with cold cream, it should only take a few minutes by hand. Work in a vigorous figure-eight pattern, switching hands if needed, until cream firmly holds marks of whisk and gently slumps into a soft, cloud-like peak as it falls off whisk. Chill cream as you wait for peach mixture to thicken.
→  Want to make perfect whipped cream every time? We got you.
When peach mixture is ready, place it next to bowl of whipped cream on your work surface. Whisk about one-third of cream into peach mixture until incorporated.

Scrape peachy cream back into bowl with plain cream. Using spatula, fold it all together until barely any streaks remain. At this point you want to work fairly quickly so cream doesn’t deflate and mousse stays nice and light.
Spoon mixture into serving glasses, cover loosely with plastic wrap, and chill until gelatin is completely set, at least 4 hours.
About 15 minutes before serving, slice 2 peaches into thin wedges and toss in a medium bowl with remaining ¼ cup jam and 1 Tbsp. lemon juice.
Spoon peach wedges and their jammy juices over set mousse and garnish with mint sprig.
Do Ahead: Mousse can be made 2 days ahead. Keep covered and chill.
